title: Third-Party Actions, Pinning, Dependency Trust, and Workflow CodeQL · Workflow Trust Lens: Ci Transitive Action Dependency Inventory
summary: A workflow uses mutable tags from unknown publishers, executes a transitive action update without review, allows untrusted workflow edits, and has no inventory or emergency revocation path. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ci transitive action dependency inventory increment in action dependency and update policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Inventory direct, reusable, composite, Docker, and nested action dependencies plus their effective privilege and provenance.
Pin third-party actions to reviewed full commit SHAs, retain readable version comments, and automate reviewed update proposals.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Predict Ci Transitive Action Dependency Inventory · Theory Ci Transitive Action Dependency Inventory
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory release uses mutable action tags, accepts automated updates without runtime review, and makes an SLSA claim from incomplete provenance. Competency probe: Inventory direct, reusable, composite, Docker, and nested action dependencies plus their effective privilege and provenance.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example release uses mutable action tags, accepts automated updates without runtime review, and makes an SLSA claim from incomplete provenance. Pin immutable identities, review diffs, verify attestations, and state non-guarantees. Competency probe: Inventory direct, reusable, composite, Docker, and nested action dependencies plus their effective privilege and provenance. A defensible solution applies Ci Transitive Action Dependency Inventory, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to action dependency and update policy.

content.code:
# Evidence: ci-worked-gha-actions-dependencies-supply-chain-3
  - competency: ci-worked-gha-actions-dependencies-supply-chain-3
    artifact: action-dependency-policy.yaml
    decision: worked gha actions dependencies supply chain 3
    environment: Node 24 TypeScript monorepo with three lockfiles
    fault-injection: expire the only retained test artifact during incident reconstruction
    retained-proof: accessible redacted timeline with owner expiry and regression control
    case-key: worked3
    change: |
      allowed-actions: reviewed-publishers
      third-party-pin: full-commit-sha
      version-comment: retained
      nested-inventory: generated
      effective-permissions: reviewed
      workflow-codeowners: delivery-security
      update: automated-pr-with-compat-tests
      analysis: codeql-actions-and-policy
      revocation: rehearsed
    verify: enumerate nested actions resolve immutable SHAs inspect bundled runtime inputs post steps permissions advisories and update evidence
    failure: Reject marketplace trust, mutable major tags, top-level-only inventory, general review assumptions, or static analysis as complete proof.
    repair: Review effective code and authority, pin immutable revisions, protect workflow changes, test updates, enforce policy, and rehearse removal.
content.caption: Runnable CI/CD workflow, artifact, release, and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

  - competency: ci-transitive-action-dependency-inventory
    artifact: action-dependency-policy.yaml
    decision: transitive action dependency inventory
    environment: reusable workflow called by three repositories at different revisions

    retained-proof: Cloud Run revision readiness smoke traffic signals abort and rollback

    failure: Reject marketplace trust, mutable major tags, top-level-only inventory, general review assumptions, or static analysis as complete proof.
